info.title: Factuarea Public API
info.description:
Public REST API for invoicing in Spain — manage invoices, quotes, proformas, delivery notes, products, clients and webhooks.

## Authentication

The API authenticates with a secret **API key**, sent on every request as:

```
Authorization: Bearer fact_live_xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx
```

or, alternatively, in the `X-API-Key` header. The key is issued from the Factuarea portal and must NEVER be exposed in public clients (browser, mobile apps).

## Environments: `live` and `test`

Each API key belongs to one of two environments, unambiguously identifiable by its **prefix**:

| Prefix | Environment | Data it operates on | External effects |
|---------|---------|---------------------------|------------------|
| `fact_live_` | **live** (production) | Your real company | Real: legal fiscal numbering, VeriFactu→AEAT, emails to clients, outbound webhooks |
| `fact_test_` | **test** (sandbox) | An isolated *sandbox* company | **Neutralized** (see below) |

The prefix is the **source of truth** for the environment: a `fact_test_` key always operates in test mode and a `fact_live_` key always in live. There is no request parameter that changes the environment — it is determined by the key.

### Data isolation (sandbox)

`fact_test_` keys operate on a dedicated **sandbox company** (a technical "twin company" of the account holder, automatically provisioned the first time test mode is used, which inherits the plan of your real company for faithful *feature-gating*). Thanks to per-company data isolation:

- Resources created with a `fact_test_` key are **not visible** from a `fact_live_` key, and vice versa.
- Test fiscal numbering uses the sandbox's own series and **never** consumes or alters the sequential numbering of your production series.

The functional surface is **identical** in both environments: the same endpoints and operations are available in test as in live.

### External effects neutralized in test

When you operate with a `fact_test_` key (sandbox environment), outbound effects are **disabled** so you can test your integration without real consequences:

- **VeriFactu**: the Alta record is created locally, but **not transmitted to AEAT**.
- **Email**: document emails are **not delivered** to real recipients.
- **Webhooks**: events are **not delivered** to your external HTTP endpoints.

In `live`, all these effects fire normally.

> **Recommendation**: always integrate and test first with a `fact_test_` key. Once your flow works, switch to the `fact_live_` prefix to operate in production.

tags.name: Clients
tags.description: Manage your customers.

tags.name: Suppliers
tags.description: Manage your suppliers and vendors.

tags.name: Invoices
tags.description: Create and manage sales invoices (compliant with Spanish AEAT VeriFactu).

tags.name: Quotes
tags.description: Create quotes and convert them to invoices.

tags.name: Proformas
tags.description: Create proforma invoices and convert them to final invoices.

tags.name: Delivery Notes
tags.description: Track goods delivered to customers; convert to invoice.

tags.name: Purchase Invoices
tags.description: Record invoices received from suppliers.

tags.name: Recurring Invoices
tags.description: Schedule invoices that auto-generate on a fixed cadence.

tags.name: Products
tags.description: Manage your product catalog.

tags.name: Webhooks
tags.description: Subscribe to events via HTTPS callbacks with HMAC-signed payloads.

tags.name: Events
tags.description: Read the event log for auditing and replay.

tags.name: Series
tags.description: Configure document numbering series (immutable by AEAT compliance).

tags.name: Taxes
tags.description: Manage configured tax rates (IVA, IGIC, IRPF) and calculate totals.

tags.name: VeriFactu
tags.description: Inspect and manage Spanish AEAT VeriFactu submissions (Ley Antifraude 11/2021).

tags.name: FacturaE
tags.description: Generate FacturaE 3.2.x XML for B2G invoicing to Spanish public administration.

tags.name: Tax Reports
tags.description: Generate Spanish tax reports (Modelo 303 IVA, Modelo 347 informativa).

tags.name: Account
tags.description: Introspect the authenticated company, plan, and API key.

tags.name: Stripe
tags.description: Auto-invoicing, connected accounts, and payouts for Stripe Connect.

post.description: Mark a proforma as accepted by the client. Returns 422 if the proforma is in a status that cannot transition to `accepted`.

parameters.name: Factuarea-Version
parameters.description: Pins the API version (`YYYY-MM-DD`, Stripe-style date versioning) for this request. When omitted, the version pinned on the API key is used, or the latest version if the key has no pin. A well-formed but unsupported version returns `400 unsupported_api_version`; a malformed value returns `400 parameter_invalid_format`. The effective version is echoed in the `Factuarea-Version` response header.

X-Request-Id.description: Unique request identifier, propagated for log correlation and support.

X-RateLimit-Limit.description: Maximum number of requests allowed in the current rate limit window (60 seconds). Omitted when the API key has no cap (unlimited tier).

X-RateLimit-Remaining.description: Number of requests remaining in the current rate limit window before receiving a 429.

X-RateLimit-Reset.description: Unix timestamp (seconds) at which the rate limit window resets and the counter returns to its maximum.

Factuarea-Version.description: Effective API version (`YYYY-MM-DD`, Stripe-style date versioning) resolved for this request: the `Factuarea-Version` request header if sent, otherwise the version pinned on the API key, otherwise the latest version. Present on every response.

post.description: Mark a quote as accepted by the client. Sets `accepted_at` to the current timestamp.

post.description: Delete several delivery notes in a single request. The body takes an `ids` array of `uuid`s. Returns a `bulk_delete_result` with the count of deleted notes and a `failed` list (`id` + Spanish `reason`) for those that could not be deleted (e.g. signed or invoiced). Supports `Idempotency-Key` for safe retries.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Public delivery note status: `draft`, `sent`, `signed`, `invoiced`, `cancelled` (the same values returned by the resource `status` field). `sent` and `signed` filter by the internal delivered state (with/without signature); use the `signed` filter as well to distinguish them precisely. Exact match on `status`.

parameters.name: status[in]
parameters.description: Public delivery note status: `draft`, `sent`, `signed`, `invoiced`, `cancelled` (the same values returned by the resource `status` field). `sent` and `signed` filter by the internal delivered state (with/without signature); use the `signed` filter as well to distinguish them precisely. Comma-separated list. Any of the values matches.

parameters.description: Client ID (UUID v7). Exact match on `client_id`.

parameters.name: client_id[in]
parameters.description: Client ID (UUID v7). Comma-separated list. Any of the values matches.

parameters.name: series_id

parameters.description: Series ID (UUID v7). Comma-separated list. Any of the values matches.

parameters.name: signed
parameters.description: Filter by signed delivery notes (`true`) or with no recorded signature (`false`). Exact match on `signed`.

parameters.name: vehicle_plate
parameters.description: License plate of the delivery vehicle (exact match). Exact match on `vehicle_plate`.

parameters.name: carrier_company
parameters.description: Transport / courier company (exact match). Exact match on `carrier_company`.

parameters.name: tags
parameters.description: Filter by classification tag (lowercase slug). Supports multiple values with `tags[in]=a,b` (JSON_CONTAINS, OR semantics — matches delivery notes carrying ANY of the tags). Exact match on `tags`.

parameters.name: tags[in]
parameters.description: Filter by classification tag (lowercase slug). Supports multiple values with `tags[in]=a,b` (JSON_CONTAINS, OR semantics — matches delivery notes carrying ANY of the tags). Comma-separated list. Any of the values matches.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Sort order. Use a field for ascending or a `-` prefix for descending (e.g. `-created`). Allowed fields: `created`, `number`, `delivery_date`. Combined with the cursor, ordering stays deterministic (a stable secondary sort by the cursor id, Stripe-style). When omitted, results follow the default cursor order (`created` descending).

post.description: Create a document numbering series. Optional `number_format` sets the numbering mask (padding + year token + separator, e.g. `{code}-{YYYY}-{00000}`); a malformed mask returns 422. Optional `initial_number` (>= 1) starts the counter at a given value to continue an existing numbering when migrating (defaults to 1). The same code may be reused across document types (multi-series). Once created, a series is immutable per AEAT compliance (the `PUT` method returns 405), so `number_format` and `initial_number` can only be set here.

delete.description: Disconnect a connected Stripe account by its `id` (UUID v7) without touching the rest of the accounts. The account is marked as `disconnected` (its already-issued invoices and history are kept; later webhooks for it are recorded without processing). Responds 204 with no body. Returns 404 (`connected_account_not_found`) if the account does not exist or belongs to another company.

put.description: Update a connected Stripe account: its `name`, the auto-invoicing `series_id` (UUID v7; send `null` to clear it and fall back to the company default series) and the per-account fiscal/auto-invoicing configuration (`autoinvoicing_enabled`, `simplified_threshold_cents` in cents within [0, 300000], `require_nif`, `refunds_enabled`, `subscription_autoinvoicing_enabled`). All fields are optional and omitted fields keep their current value. Returns 403 (`feature_not_available_in_plan`) when the plan does not include the Stripe module, 404 (`connected_account_not_found`) if the account does not exist or belongs to another company, and 422 (`invalid_autoinvoicing_series`) when the series does not exist, belongs to another company or is not an invoice series.

get.description: Download the PDF representation of a delivery note. Returns the binary PDF stream (`application/pdf`). Pass `?download=1` for `Content-Disposition: attachment` (file download); otherwise it is served `inline`. The response carries an `ETag`; resend it via `If-None-Match` to receive `304 Not Modified` when the document is unchanged.

parameters.name: download
parameters.description: Cuando es truthy (`1`/`true`), fuerza `Content-Disposition: attachment` (descarga de fichero) en lugar de `inline`.

parameters.name: Factuarea-Version
parameters.description: Pins the API version (`YYYY-MM-DD`, Stripe-style date versioning) for this request. When omitted, the version pinned on the API key is used, or the latest version if the key has no pin. A well-formed but unsupported version returns `400 unsupported_api_version`; a malformed value returns `400 parameter_invalid_format`. The effective version is echoed in the `Factuarea-Version` response header.

200.description: Binary PDF stream of the delivery note (`application/pdf`). Use `?download=1` to receive `Content-Disposition: attachment` (forces a file download); otherwise the disposition is `inline`. The response carries an `ETag`; send it back via `If-None-Match` to get a `304 Not Modified` when the PDF has not changed.

ETag.description: Entity tag of the PDF (`sha1` of uuid + updated_at). Send it back in `If-None-Match` to leverage the 304 cache.

Content-Disposition.description: Disposition of the PDF: `inline; filename="…"` by default, or `attachment; filename="…"` when `?download=1` is passed (RFC 5987 `filename*` included for UTF-8 names).

get.description: Streams the FacturaE **3.2.2** XML for the invoice (B2G compliance), XSD-conformant with full tax breakdown. When the company has an active signing certificate the body is signed **XAdES-EPES** (Facturae v3.1 signature policy) and the filename is `.xsig`; without a certificate the XML is returned unsigned as `.xml`. The `X-Facturae-Signed: true|false` response header tells both cases apart. Draft invoices return 422 `invoice_not_emittable_for_facturae`.

post.description: Look up a series by its `code` (sent in the JSON body, case-insensitive). A `code` is NOT globally unique: the same `code` may be reused across different `document_type`s (multi-series — e.g. one `2026` series for invoices and another `2026` for quotes). Therefore: pass `document_type` to disambiguate and resolve the exact `(code, document_type)` series. If you omit `document_type`, the code is matched across all document types ordered by `uuid` ascending — when exactly one series matches it is returned, but when several match (ambiguous code) the request returns 422 `document_type_required_for_ambiguous_code` (it does NOT silently pick one). Returns the matching series, or 404 if none exists within your company.

get.description: Returns the Stripe Connect integration status and auto-invoicing configuration for your company: whether Stripe is connected, whether auto-invoicing is enabled, the series used (`series_id`), the effective plan gating (`available_for_current_plan`, `required_plans`), the fiscal policy: `simplified_threshold_cents` (the threshold in cents below which charges without a tax ID are auto-issued as simplified invoices), `require_nif` (whether a tax ID is required, vetoing automatic simplified invoices), `refunds_enabled` (whether a Stripe refund automatically generates a linked corrective invoice), and `subscription_autoinvoicing_enabled` (whether Stripe subscription cycles are auto-invoiced). This company-wide endpoint reflects the single connected account when exactly one is connected; with multiple connected accounts it returns 422 (`per_account_config_required`) — read each account via `GET /v1/connected-accounts`.

put.description: Enable or disable auto-invoicing of Stripe Connect charges and choose the series used by the auto-created invoices. You can also tune the fiscal policy: `simplified_threshold_cents` (optional, range [0, 300000] = 0–3,000 €), `require_nif` (optional boolean), `refunds_enabled` (optional boolean — whether a Stripe refund generates an automatic corrective invoice) and `subscription_autoinvoicing_enabled` (optional boolean — whether Stripe subscription cycles are auto-invoiced; requires `enabled` to also be on). All optional fields keep the current values when omitted. This company-wide endpoint proxies to the single connected account when exactly one is connected; with multiple connected accounts it returns 422 (`per_account_config_required`) — configure each account via `PUT /v1/connected-accounts/{account}`. Returns 403 (`feature_not_available_in_plan`) when enabling without a sufficient plan, 422 (`validation_error`) when the threshold is out of range, and 422 (`stripe_not_connected`) when Stripe is not connected.

The file is validated by magic bytes (ASN.1 DER) and capped at 100 KB; the password is encrypted at rest. The uploaded certificate is activated automatically (previous ones are deactivated). The `Location` header points to `/v1/verifactu/certificates/active`.

get.description: List the closed catalog of event types that Factuarea can emit to webhook endpoints. Use it to populate a subscription UI instead of hard-coding event names. Each entry exposes its `name`, `category` (derived from the `<category>.*` prefix), a Spanish `description`, and a `status`: `available` means the type is emitted today and may be subscribed to via `enabled_events`; `coming_soon` means the type is reserved for a future release — it is listed for discovery but is NOT yet subscribable (passing it in `enabled_events` returns 422). The set of currently emitted (`available`) types is the closed source of truth `EventName::CATALOG`.

get.description: List events in your event log with cursor-based pagination. Each event records something that happened in your account (an invoice was paid, a quote accepted, …) and is the same object delivered to your webhook endpoints. Supports filtering by `type[in]` and `created[gte|lte]`.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Number of objects to return. Integer between 1 and 100. Defaults to 25.

parameters.name: starting_after
parameters.description: Cursor for forward pagination. Use the `uuid` of the last object on the previous page.

parameters.name: ending_before
parameters.description: Cursor for backward pagination. Use the `uuid` of the first object on the current page.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Event type (e.g. invoice.created). Exact match on `type`.

parameters.name: type[in]
parameters.description: Event type (e.g. invoice.created). Comma-separated list. Any of the values matches.

parameters.name: created[gte]
parameters.description: Creation date (ISO 8601). Greater than or equal to the given value.

parameters.name: created[lte]
parameters.description: Creation date (ISO 8601). Less than or equal to the given value.

parameters.name: created[gt]
parameters.description: Creation date (ISO 8601). Strictly greater than the given value.

parameters.name: created[lt]
parameters.description: Creation date (ISO 8601). Strictly less than the given value.

post.description: Submits an issued invoice to FACe (the Spanish B2G general entry point). Requires the client's three DIR3 codes and an active signing certificate; the FacturaE 3.2.2 XML is signed XAdES-EPES and presented through the FACe web service, returning the registry number. No request body: the invoice travels in the path and the DIR3 codes are read from the client. Test keys (`fact_test_`) simulate the submission (`FACE-SANDBOX-*` registry) without contacting FACe. Errors: 422 (`invoice_not_emittable_for_facturae`, `client_missing_dir3_codes`, `signing_certificate_required`), 409 `face_submission_already_exists` (an active submission already exists for the invoice), 502 `face_transmission_failed` (nothing is persisted when the FACe web service is down).

get.description: List the Stripe payouts ingested for your company, with cursor-based pagination. Each payout exposes the net/fees/gross amounts, currency, arrival date, reconciliation `status` (`ingested`/`reconciled`) and an informative `composition` (component charges + fees reported by Stripe). Filter by `status` and by arrival-date window (`arrival_date[gte]`/`arrival_date[lte]`). Payouts are read-only on the public API; bank reconciliation happens in the dashboard.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Number of objects to return. Integer between 1 and 100. Defaults to 25.

parameters.name: starting_after
parameters.description: Cursor for forward pagination. Use the `uuid` of the last object on the previous page.

parameters.name: ending_before
parameters.description: Cursor for backward pagination. Use the `uuid` of the first object on the current page.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Reconciliation status (ingested, reconciled). Exact match on `status`.

parameters.name: status[in]
parameters.description: Reconciliation status (ingested, reconciled). Comma-separated list. Any of the values matches.

parameters.name: arrival_date[gte]
parameters.description: Expected arrival date of the payout (YYYY-MM-DD). Greater than or equal to the given value.

parameters.name: arrival_date[lte]
parameters.description: Expected arrival date of the payout (YYYY-MM-DD). Less than or equal to the given value.

parameters.name: arrival_date[gt]
parameters.description: Expected arrival date of the payout (YYYY-MM-DD). Strictly greater than the given value.

parameters.name: arrival_date[lt]
parameters.description: Expected arrival date of the payout (YYYY-MM-DD). Strictly less than the given value.

get.description:
Retrieve a series by its `uuid`.

Series are **immutable** for fiscal compliance (AEAT VeriFactu — legal numbering continuity): `PUT`, `PATCH` and `DELETE` on `/v1/series/{uuid}` return `405 Method Not Allowed` with `error.code = "series_immutable"` and header `Allow: GET, POST`. To "delete" a series use `POST /v1/series/{uuid}/archive`; to change the numbering, create a new series and mark it as default.

post.description: Record a handwritten signature on a delivery note (typically captured from the recipient on delivery). The signature image must be a base64-encoded **PNG** (≤2 MB) — JPEG/SVG are rejected with 422 `invalid_param_format` (decision D17, PNG-only in v1). On success `signed_at`/`signed_by` are updated; signing records the signature data but does not change the delivery note `status` (it stays `delivered`). The signature audit log retains hashed recipient PII for 5 years to satisfy Spanish LSSI-CE retention obligations; use `POST /v1/delivery_notes/signature-audits/{auditId}/forget` to honor a GDPR Art. 17 erasure request. Supports `Idempotency-Key` for safe retries.

post.description: Corrects (subsana) an AEAT-rejected VeriFactu record: regenerates the correctable content from the source invoice keeping the original `huella`, resets the transmission round, and re-queues the AEAT transmission (202). Returns 404 if the record does not exist, 422 `business_rule_violation` with subcode `record_not_rejected` if the record is not in rejected status, or `requires_annulment` when the correction affects fingerprint fields (annul + new alta required instead).

Public REST API v1 — POST /v1/proformas/{uuid}/accept.

Body opcional: `reason` (string ≤500), `metadata` (objeto ≤50 keys,
≤500 chars/valor — validado con el VO `Metadata`).
AcceptProformaRequest.title: AcceptProformaRequest

AcceptQuoteRequest.description:
Public REST API v1 — POST /v1/quotes/{uuid}/accept.

Optional body: `accepted_on` (date, defaults to today), `notes`.
The controller performs the cross-field validation for `quote_expired`
(`valid_until < today` → 422).
AcceptQuoteRequest.title: AcceptQuoteRequest

Account.description: Snapshot of the company, plan, developer addon status and metadata of the API key used to make the request. Use this endpoint to introspect credentials and discover limits with a single call.

object.description: Stripe-like discriminator. Always `account` for this resource.

addon.description: State of the `developer_api` addon for this company.

active.description: true when the addon is currently usable (paid or within grace period).

in_grace.description: true when the subscription lapsed but the grace period is still open.

expires_at.description: Grace period cutoff (ISO 8601) when `in_grace=true`; null otherwise.

api_key.description: Metadata of the API key used to authenticate the request. The secret is never returned (it is only shown once at creation time).

name.description: Human-friendly label assigned at creation time.

prefix.description: First chars of the key (e.g. `fact_live_1N0Fnyhh`) — safe to log.

scopes.description: Authorized scopes. `*` means super scope (full access).

Account.title: Account

AeatAccessRecord.description: A dissociated (anonymized) AEAT access record (REQ-VF-132). Third-party tax identifiers, email and IP are never exposed in plain text — only an anonymized hash.

id.description: UUID (v7) del billing record subyacente (usado solo para ordenar/cursorear).

accessed_at.description: Timestamp of generation/dissociation of the record.

accessor_identifier_hash.description: Hash SHA-256 anonimizado del identificador del emisor (no se expone el NIF en claro).

record_count_disclosed.description: Number of dissociated records in this entry.

AeatAccessRecord.title: AeatAccessRecord

AlternativeId.description: Alternative tax identification for non-Spanish clients. Mutually exclusive with `tax_id` (NIF/CIF/NIE).

type.description: Type of identification: passport, foreign ID, foreign tax identification or none.

country_code.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code in uppercase (other than `ES`).

AlternativeId.title: AlternativeId

AnnulInvoiceV1Request.description:
Public REST API v1 — POST /v1/invoices/{uuid}/annul.

Body: `reason` (string, required, max. 500). The reason is persisted
on the Invoice aggregate and included in the VeriFactu cancellation record
(AnulacionRecord) when applicable.
AnnulInvoiceV1Request.title: AnnulInvoiceV1Request

AttachPurchaseInvoiceFileRequest.description:
Public REST API v1 — POST /v1/purchase_invoices/{uuid}/attach-file.

Multipart upload: `file` field.

This FormRequest only validates the basic STRUCTURE (presence, that it is a
valid uploaded file and a COARSE safety size cap — 50 MB — to cut off
abusive uploads before touching the Vault). The canonical domain validation
— exact MIME allowlist and configurable size
(`config('document.attachment.max_size_mb')`, 10 MB), filename sanitization
against path traversal — is performed by the Shared VO `AttachmentRef::create()`,
which emits the canonical exception `InvalidAttachmentException`
(`PublicApiMappable` → HTTP 422 with subcodes `attachment_mime_not_allowed`
/ `attachment_too_large` / `attachment_invalid_filename`).

No `mimetypes` rules nor a `max` aligned with the domain limit are declared
here: doing so would shadow the error catalog (an invalid MIME/size would
yield Laravel's generic `invalid_param_value` instead of the canonical VO
subcode). The coarse 50 MB cap never collides with the real 10 MB limit, so
the VO always gets to emit `attachment_too_large` for files between 10 and
50 MB while the safety guard still cuts off genuinely abusive uploads.
AttachPurchaseInvoiceFileRequest.title: AttachPurchaseInvoiceFileRequest

BankAccount.description: Cuenta bancaria de un cliente. El IBAN es obligatorio; el resto de campos son opcionales.

is_default.description: Marca la cuenta como predeterminada para domiciliaciones.

BankAccount.title: BankAccount

BulkDeleteClientsRequest.description:
Public REST API v1 — POST /v1/clients/bulk-delete (canónico).

Body: `{ ids: string[] }`. Acepta entre 1 y 200 UUIDs. La validación
de pertenencia al tenant la realiza el Handler (filtrado por company_id);
los UUIDs ajenos se ignoran silenciosamente y aparecerán en `failed`.
BulkDeleteClientsRequest.title: BulkDeleteClientsRequest

BulkDeleteDeliveryNotesRequest.description:
Public REST API v1 — POST /v1/delivery_notes/bulk-delete.

Body requerido: `ids` (array de UUIDs entre 1 y 100). Los UUIDs deben
ser strings con formato UUID; la pertenencia al tenant se verifica en el
controller (los UUIDs desconocidos o cross-tenant se reportan como
fallidos en la respuesta, no como 404 global).
BulkDeleteDeliveryNotesRequest.title: BulkDeleteDeliveryNotesRequest

BulkDeleteProductsRequest.description:
Public REST API v1 — POST /v1/products/bulk-delete.

Body: `{ ids: string[] }`. Accepts between 1 and 200 IDs (UUID v7). Tenant
membership validation is performed by the Handler (filtered by company_id);
foreign IDs are silently ignored and will appear in `skipped`.
BulkDeleteProductsRequest.title: BulkDeleteProductsRequest

BulkDeletePurchaseInvoicesRequest.description:
Public REST API v1 — POST /v1/purchase_invoices/bulk-delete (canónico). El
alias legacy `DELETE /v1/purchase_invoices/bulk` se eliminó en el change
`public-api-official-sdks` (P0 1.3).

Body requerido: `ids` (array de UUIDs entre 1 y 100). La pertenencia al
tenant se valida en el controller — UUIDs desconocidos se reportan como
fallidos.
BulkDeletePurchaseInvoicesRequest.title: BulkDeletePurchaseInvoicesRequest

BulkDeleteRecurringInvoicesRequest.description:
Public REST API v1 — POST /v1/recurring_invoices/bulk-delete.

Body requerido: `ids` (array de UUIDs entre 1 y 100). La pertenencia al
tenant se resuelve en el Handler — UUIDs desconocidos o de otra company se
reportan en `failed[]` (defense-in-depth contra enumeration cross-tenant).

La key del payload es `ids` (alineado con PurchaseInvoice/Quote/Supplier y
el spec `public-api-recurring-invoice-bulk-delete-cqrs`).
BulkDeleteRecurringInvoicesRequest.title: BulkDeleteRecurringInvoicesRequest

BulkDeleteResult.description: Result of a bulk-delete operation. Indicates how many resources were deleted successfully and the list of failures (id + reason in Spanish). Shape shared by all `/v1/{resource}/bulk-delete` endpoints of the public API.

failed.description: Resources that could not be deleted, with the reason in Spanish (internal messages are not exposed).

id.description: UUID (v7) del recurso que no pudo eliminarse.

reason.description: Reason for the failure, in Spanish.

example.reason: El cliente tiene facturas emitidas y no se puede eliminar.
El cliente tiene presupuestos asociados y no se puede eliminar.

examples.reason: El cliente tiene facturas emitidas y no se puede eliminar.
El cliente tiene presupuestos asociados y no se puede eliminar.

BulkDeleteResult.title: BulkDeleteResult

BulkDeleteSuppliersRequest.description:
Public REST API v1 — POST /v1/suppliers/bulk-delete (canónico).

Body: `{ ids: string[] }`. Acepta entre 1 y 200 identificadores (valor
UUID v7). La validación de pertenencia al tenant la realiza el Handler
(filtrado por company_id); los identificadores ajenos se ignoran
silenciosamente y aparecerán en `failed`.
BulkDeleteSuppliersRequest.title: BulkDeleteSuppliersRequest

BulkUpdateProductStockRequest.description:
Public REST API v1 — POST /v1/products/bulk-update-stock.

Body: `{ updates: [{ product_id: string, stock: int, operation?: 'set'|'add'|'subtract' }] }`.
Accepts up to 500 updates in a single operation.

We accept `items` as an alias of the canonical `updates` field for
forgiveness with integrators following the most common convention. The
controller normalizes it to `updates`.
BulkUpdateProductStockRequest.title: BulkUpdateProductStockRequest

CalculateTaxRequest.description:
Public REST API v1 — POST /v1/taxes/calculate.

Body: `{ base: float, taxes_id: string }`. `taxes_id` es la FK a la tabla
global `taxes` (valor UUID v7) — plural (D1), NUNCA `tax_id` (NIF/CIF fiscal).
Devuelve `{ base, tax_rate, tax_amount, total_amount, tax }`.

taxes_id.description:
NOTE: no `exists:taxes,uuid` rule here. Existence is resolved by the
Controller via `resolveUuid(TaxModel::class)`, which throws
`TaxNotFoundException` (404 `tax_not_found`) for a well-formed but
non-existent uuid — via `PublicApiNotFoundExceptionRegistry`. With
`exists` the FormRequest would emit 422 `invalid_param_value` and the
canonical 404 would never be reached. The `uuid` rule keeps the 422 for
malformed uuids (arbitrary text is not passed to the database lookup).

CalculateTaxRequest.title: CalculateTaxRequest

CalculateTotalsRequest.description:
Public REST API v1 — POST /v1/taxes/calculate-totals.

Body: `{ lines: [{ quantity, unit_price, discount?, vat_rate?,
retention_rate?, surcharge_rate? }] }`. Returns subtotal, VAT, surcharge,
withholding and total.

The canonical field is `unit_price` (aligned with Invoice/Quote lines).
`price` is accepted as a legacy alias so as not to break integrators that
already send the previous shape; the controller normalizes it to `unit_price`.
CalculateTotalsRequest.title: CalculateTotalsRequest

CanAnnulInvoice.description: Result of the pre-cancellation validator of an invoice: whether it can be cancelled, the blocking reasons (when it cannot) and whether the cancellation will create an additional VeriFactu record.

can_annul.description: Indica si la factura puede anularse en su estado actual.

reasons.description: Blocking reasons when `can_annul` is `false`. Empty `[]` when the invoice can be cancelled.
example: La factura está en estado Borrador y no puede ser anulada
examples: La factura está en estado Borrador y no puede ser anulada

will_create_verifactu.description: Indicates whether the cancellation will generate a cancellation record in VeriFactu.

info.description: Additional informational messages about the cancellation (non-blocking warnings). Empty `[]` when there are none.
example: La factura no tiene registro VeriFactu de alta. Se anulará sin registro VeriFactu.
examples: La factura no tiene registro VeriFactu de alta. Se anulará sin registro VeriFactu.

CanAnnulInvoice.title: CanAnnulInvoice

CancelFaceSubmissionV1Request.description:
Public REST API v1 — POST /v1/face-submissions/{faceSubmission}/cancel.

The cancellation reason (`reason`) is required: it travels to the FACe
web service alongside the cancellation request (code 4200). Same contract
as the SPA surface (`CancelFaceSubmissionRequest`).
CancelFaceSubmissionV1Request.title: CancelFaceSubmissionV1Request

CensusVerification.description: Result of verifying the account's persisted company name + tax ID pair against the AEAT census (VNifV2). Use it to anticipate VeriFactu 4104 rejections before invoicing. Fail-open: returns `unavailable` when AEAT cannot be reached.

object.description: Stripe-like discriminator. Always `census_verification` for this resource.

status.description: Census result. `identified`: name + tax ID match an active taxpayer. `not_identified`: the pair is not in the census. `not_identified_similar`: a similar individual exists (natural persons only). `identified_inactive` / `identified_revoked`: the taxpayer is deregistered or revoked. `unavailable`: AEAT could not answer (timeout, fault, no platform certificate) — verification is informational and never blocks.

verified_name.description: The company name that was checked against the census (the persisted account name). `null` when the account has never been verified.

checked_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the last verification. `null` when never verified.

CensusVerification.title: CensusVerification

ChainValidation.description: Result of recomputing and verifying the VeriFactu hash chain (`huella`) of your company without mutating data. Returned by `GET /v1/verifactu/chain/validate`.

is_valid.description: Indicates whether the hash chain is intact.

first_invalid_record_id.description: UUID (v7) of the first invalid record, or `null` if the chain is intact.

ChainValidation.title: ChainValidation

commercial_name.description: Nombre comercial (DBA) opcional, distinto del nombre fiscal.

billing_emails.description: Additional emails for invoice delivery (administration, accounting). Maximum 5.

coordinates.description: Geographic coordinates of the client. `null` when not recorded.

default_discount.description: Descuento por defecto aplicable al cliente (porcentaje).

default_vat_rate.description: Tipo de IVA por defecto aplicable al cliente (porcentaje).

is_surcharge_subject.description: Indica si al cliente se le aplica recargo de equivalencia.

bank_accounts.description: Bank accounts associated with the client. Empty `[]` when there are none.

preferred_operation_regime.description: Preferred operation regime of the client for VAT / VeriFactu purposes.

accumulate_347.description: Whether this client accumulates towards the annual Modelo 347 report (operations with third parties above the legal threshold).

dir3_accounting_office.description: DIR3 code of the public-administration accounting office (Oficina Contable). FACe/Facturae directory code, not a foreign key. `null` for non-AAPP clients.

dir3_managing_body.description: DIR3 code of the public-administration managing body (Órgano Gestor). FACe/Facturae directory code, not a foreign key. `null` for non-AAPP clients.

dir3_processing_unit.description: DIR3 code of the public-administration processing unit (Unidad Tramitadora). FACe/Facturae directory code, not a foreign key. `null` for non-AAPP clients.

external_id.description: External integration key (ERP/CRM/e-commerce) mapping this client to a record in a third-party system. Free-format, unique per company, distinct from the fiscal `tax_id`.

ClientActivity.description: Un evento del timeline de actividad de un cliente. Agrupa eventos de dominio originados por cambios sobre el propio cliente y por documentos que lo referencian (facturas, presupuestos, etc.).

event_type.description: Tipo de evento de dominio (p. ej. `client.updated`, `invoice.created`).

description.description: Human-readable description of the event in Spanish.
description.example: Se actualizó el correo electrónico del cliente.
examples: Se actualizó el correo electrónico del cliente.

metadata.description: Metadatos del evento. Los identificadores internos (PKs) se eliminan; los `*_uuid` se conservan.

type.description: Type of actor that originated the event: `user` for a human in the internal app, `api_key` for an action performed through the public v1 API.

id.description: UUID (v7) of the actor: the user when `type=user`, or the API key when `type=api_key`.

name.description: Nombre del actor en el momento del evento: nombre del usuario o nombre de la API key. `null` si no se pudo resolver.

performed_by.description: Actor that originated the event. `{type:"user",...}` for an internal user, `{type:"api_key",...}` when performed via the public v1 API, or `null` when the event is system-generated (scheduler, periodic sweep) with no attributable actor.

ClientActivity.title: ClientActivity

ClientStats.description: Aggregated summary of the client portfolio of the authenticated company: counters, invoiced and pending amounts, and average metrics. Returned by `GET /v1/clients/stats`.

total.description: Total de clientes registrados en la empresa.

with_email.description: Clients with a registered email address.

with_phone.description: Clients with a registered phone number.

with_invoices.description: Clientes que tienen al menos una factura emitida.

with_pending_invoices.description: Clientes con al menos una factura pendiente de cobro.

pending_invoices_amount.description: Importe agregado de facturas pendientes de cobro (EUR).

new_this_month.description: Clientes nuevos creados durante el mes en curso.

ClientStats.title: ClientStats

CompanyCertificate.description: Metadata of an FNMT (PKCS#12) certificate used to sign VeriFactu transmissions. The certificate password and binary are never exposed — only X.509 metadata.

id.description: UUID (v7) del certificado.

subject_nif.description: NIF del titular del certificado X.509.

subject_kind.description: Type of holder (individual / legal entity / representative).

is_active.description: Indica si es el certificado activo para firmar transmisiones.

revoked_at.description: Revocation date, or `null` if not revoked.

CompanyCertificate.title: CompanyCertificate

ConnectedAccount.description: A connected Stripe account (Stripe Connect, multi-store) for your company. The public `id` is the account UUID (v7). `external_account_id` (`acct_xxx`) is the external Stripe id, not a foreign key. `series_id` is the UUID (v7) of the auto-invoicing document series (`null` means the company default series). The configuration fields are the effective per-account auto-invoicing settings.

id.description: UUID (v7) of the connected account. Public identity (KEY `id`).

object.description: Stripe-like discriminator. Always `connected_account` for this resource.

name.description: Editable display name of the connected account.

external_account_id.description: External Stripe connected account id (`acct_xxx`). NOT an internal UUID nor a foreign key.

external_account_name.description: Account name reported by Stripe, or `null` if unknown.

series_id.description: UUID (v7) of the document series used for invoices auto-created from this account. `null` means the company default series is used.

autoinvoicing_enabled.description: Whether auto-invoicing of charges on this account is enabled. Defaults to `false`.

simplified_threshold_cents.description: Simplified invoice (F2) threshold in cents for this account, range `[0, 300000]` (0–3,000 €). Defaults to 40000 (400 €).

require_nif.description: Whether a Spanish tax ID (NIF) is required for auto-invoicing on this account. Defaults to `false`.

refunds_enabled.description: Whether a Stripe refund (`charge.refunded`) on this account automatically generates a linked corrective invoice. Defaults to `true`.

subscription_autoinvoicing_enabled.description: Whether Stripe subscription cycles on this account are auto-invoiced. Defaults to `false`.

status.description: Account status. `active` while connected; `disconnected` (terminal) after disconnecting (invoices and history are kept).

connected_at.description: When the account was connected (ISO 8601), or `null` if unknown.

ConnectedAccount.title: ConnectedAccount

ConvertDeliveryNoteRequest.description:
Public REST API v1 — POST /v1/delivery_notes/{uuid}/convert.

Required body: `target` — only `invoice` is supported. The DeliveryNote
BC only exposes `ConvertDeliveryNoteToInvoiceCommand`, so the validation
rejects any other target with a 422 before reaching the controller.

Optional body: `target_series_id` — UUID of a `series` of type `invoice`
belonging to the caller's tenant. Directs the numbering of the generated
invoice. When omitted, the conversion keeps the default-series behavior.
The existence check is scoped to the caller's `company_id` (TenantRule), so
a series of another tenant or a non-invoice series is rejected with 422.
ConvertDeliveryNoteRequest.title: ConvertDeliveryNoteRequest

ConvertProformaRequest.description:
Public REST API v1 — POST /v1/proformas/{uuid}/convert.

Required body: `target` ∈ {invoice}. Only conversion to invoice is
supported — other targets (`proforma`, `delivery_note`) do NOT apply
because a proforma can only be converted to an invoice by BC design.
ConvertProformaRequest.title: ConvertProformaRequest

ConvertQuoteRequest.description:
Public REST API v1 — POST /v1/quotes/{uuid}/convert.

Body requerido: `target` ∈ {invoice, proforma, delivery_note}.
Body opcional: `issued_on` (date), `due_on` (date).
ConvertQuoteRequest.title: ConvertQuoteRequest

CreateClientRequest.description:
Public REST API v1 — POST /v1/clients.

Required body: `name`. Optional: `tax_id`, `vat_id`, `email`, `phone`,
`address` (object with sub-keys `line1`, `line2`, `number`, `floor`,
`door`, `staircase`, `postal_code`, `city`, `province`, `country`),
`notes`, `metadata`, `commercial_name`, `contact_person`, `fax`, `mobile`,
`website`, `billing_emails[]`, `default_discount`, `default_vat_rate`,
`default_retention_rate`, `is_surcharge_subject`, `accumulate_347`,
`bank_accounts[]`, `latitude`, `longitude`, `preferred_operation_regime`,
`payment_method`, `payment_terms_days`,
`alternative_id` (object `{type, value, country_code}`).

The validation of `metadata` invariants (≤50 keys, ≤500 chars/value) is
performed by the Handler via `Metadata::create()`. The validation of domain
invariants (XOR `tax_id`/`alternative_id`, direct_debit ⇒ default bank
account, billing_emails without duplicates) is performed by the `Client`
aggregate. The typed exceptions (`MetadataTooManyKeysException`,
`MetadataValueTooLongException`, `InvalidTaxIdException`,
`InvalidAlternativeIdException`, `InvalidBillingEmailsException`,
`InvalidClientStateException`) implement `PublicApiMappable` and propagate
to the `ExceptionRenderer` with the canonical v1 envelope.

`tax_id` is de facto required unless `alternative_id` is sent. The
FormRequest leaves it `nullable` and delegates validation to the aggregate,
which throws `InvalidTaxIdException` (422 `tax_id_required`) if both are missing.
CreateClientRequest.title: CreateClientRequest

CreateCorrectiveInvoiceRequest.description:
Public REST API v1 — POST /v1/invoices/{uuid}/corrective.

Body:
 - `correction_reason` (canonical slug BR-INV-018) — required field. Maps to
   a fine-grained VeriFactu code R1..R4 (error_fundado→R1, concurso→R2,
   incobrable→R3, rest→R4). Replaces the former free-text `reason` that the
   controller hardcoded to `'otras'` (fiscal bug).
 - `correction_type` (`full` or `partial`) — canonical field.
 - `corrective_type` — deprecated alias kept for backward compatibility.
   If sent and `correction_type` is absent, it is promoted automatically.
 - `notes` (optional string) — free-text traceability.
 - `lines` (array, required if `correction_type=partial`).
CreateCorrectiveInvoiceRequest.title: CreateCorrectiveInvoiceRequest

CreateDeliveryNoteRequest.description:
Public REST API v1 — POST /v1/delivery_notes.

Required body: `client_id`, `lines[]` (min 1). Optional:
`series_id`, `delivery_date`, `notes`, `internal_notes`,
`reference_number`, `transport_details`, `delivery_address` &c,
`metadata` (≤50 keys, ≤500 chars/value — VO `Metadata`), `tags` (lista de
slugs, ≤40 c/u, ≤30 — VO `DocumentTagCollection`), `custom_fields` (lista
tipada `[{field,value}]`, `field` ≤60, `value` ≤500, ≤50 — VO `CustomFields`).

vehicle_plate.description: Logistics extension (semantic format validated in the Aggregate)

CreateDeliveryNoteRequest.title: CreateDeliveryNoteRequest

CreateInvoiceRequest.description:
Public REST API v1 — POST /v1/invoices.

Required body: `client_id`, `series_id`, `issued_on`, `due_on`,
`lines[]` (min 1). Optional: `notes`, `metadata` (≤50 keys, ≤500
chars/value — VO `Metadata`), `tags` (lista de slugs, ≤40 c/u, ≤30 — VO
`DocumentTagCollection`), `custom_fields` (lista tipada `[{field,value}]`,
`field` ≤60, `value` ≤500, ≤50 — VO `CustomFields`).
CreateInvoiceRequest.title: CreateInvoiceRequest

CreateProductRequest.description:
Public REST API v1 — POST /v1/products.

Required body: `name`, `price`. Optional: `sku`, `description`, `tags`,
`stock` (initial quantity), `low_stock_threshold` (per-product),
`currency` (EUR only — Producto is read-only EUR; any other code → 422),
`tax_rate_id`, `is_active`, `metadata`, `external_id`.
Validation of `metadata` via VO `Metadata`.

sku.description:
`sku` uniqueness per company is NOT validated here — the Aggregate
throws `DuplicateSkuException` and the controller translates it to 409
`sku_already_exists` (not 422). Validating here would give 422 and break
the external-api-products spec contract.

stock.description:
Stock inicial (solo en create; la mutación posterior vive en
`PUT /v1/products/{uuid}/stock`). Ausente → default 0 en el Mapper.

currency.description:
Producto es read-only EUR: solo se admite `EUR` (o ausencia/null).
Cualquier otra moneda → 422 (antes se aceptaba-y-descartaba).

tax_rate_id.description:
`taxes` is a global system catalog (without a `company_id` column).
Do NOT use TenantRule here — it would add `WHERE company_id = X` against a
table without that column and cause a 500 (SQLSTATE 42S22). Global
validation by uuid, like in the rest of the BCs (DeliveryNote V1, etc.).

external_id.description:
Clave de integración ERP/CRM de terceros (ortogonal al `sku`). La
unicidad por empresa NO se valida aquí: el UPSERT por `external_id`
(controller) reutiliza el registro existente. Free-format ≤100.

CreateProductRequest.title: CreateProductRequest

CreateProformaRequest.description:
Public REST API v1 — POST /v1/proformas.

Required body: `client_id`, `issued_on`, `lines[]` (min 1). Optional:
`series_id`, `valid_until`, `notes`, `terms_and_conditions`, `metadata`
(≤50 keys, ≤500 chars/value — VO `Metadata`), `tags` (lista de slugs, ≤40
c/u, ≤30 — VO `DocumentTagCollection`), `custom_fields` (lista tipada
`[{field,value}]`, `field` ≤60, `value` ≤500, ≤50 — VO `CustomFields`).
CreateProformaRequest.title: CreateProformaRequest

CreatePurchaseInvoiceRequest.description:
Public REST API v1 — POST /v1/purchase_invoices.

Required body: `supplier_id`, `external_invoice_number`,
`issued_on`, `lines[]` (min 1). Optional: `due_on`, `received_on`,
`internal_code`, `notes`, `metadata` (≤50 keys, ≤500 chars/value —
VO `Metadata`).

`external_invoice_number` is the number the supplier puts on their
invoice (it is not one of our series). The combination
`(company_id, external_invoice_number)` is UNIQUE in the database.

We accept `supplier_invoice_number` as a deprecated alias of the
canonical `external_invoice_number` (more readable for integrators
used to Stripe/Holded).

Conflict (409): if `external_invoice_number` already exists for the
caller's company, the create responds `409` with the canonical v1
envelope `error.code === "resource_already_exists"`,
`error.subcode === "purchase_invoice_already_exists"`,
`error.param === "external_invoice_number"` — never a generic `500`.
The conflict is raised by `DuplicatePurchaseInvoiceNumberException`
(defense-in-depth: pre-check + UNIQUE
`purchase_invoices_company_real_number_unique`). The same number in a
different company is NOT a duplicate (`201`).

external_id.description:
Clave de negocio externa (clave de integración ERP/CRM, change
external-id-documents). Ortogonal a `external_invoice_number` (el
número fiscal del proveedor): `external_id` es el ID del recurso
en el sistema del integrador, único por empresa.

status.description:
Optional initial status (BR-PUR-001). 4-state model:
CREATION allowlist `draft|pending` (`received`/`pending_payment`
were merged into `pending`). `paid|cancelled` are lifecycle
transitions (mark_paid/change_status), NOT creation states.
If omitted, the domain applies the default `draft`.

internal_notes.description:
Extend fields. Basic SHAPE only; the `payment_method` allowlist,
`tax_period` format and `tags` cardinality are validated by the
VO/Aggregate.

operation_class.description:
`operation_class` (BR-PUR-018 / BR-TXR-030) SÍ es escribible vía API
v1 en compras: el integrador clasifica el origen de su gasto a
efectos del IVA soportado del Modelo 303 (casillas [28]-[39]).
Conjunto cerrado; nullable → default `corriente`.

exclude_347.description:
`exclude_347` (BR-TXR-024) SÍ es escribible vía API v1 en compras:
el integrador clasifica sus propios gastos. En ventas (Invoice) el
flag es de solo lectura en v1 y solo se muta desde la SPA interna
(ver InvoiceV1Resource). Asimetría deliberada por superficie.

custom_fields.description:
custom_fields tipados `[{field,value}]` (§10) — VO Shared
`CustomFields`. `field` ≤60 no vacío, `value` ≤500, ≤50 entradas.

retention_rate.description:
Retención IRPF e IVA deducible por línea (BR-PUR-022). `retention_rate`
= porcentaje practicado al proveedor (0–100); `vat_deductible` marca
la deducibilidad del IVA (informativo, no altera el total pagado).

CreatePurchaseInvoiceRequest.title: CreatePurchaseInvoiceRequest

CreateQuoteRequest.description:
Public REST API v1 — POST /v1/quotes.

Required body: `client_id`, `series_id`, `issued_on`, `valid_until`,
`lines[]` (min 1). Optional: `notes`, `terms`, `metadata` (≤50 keys,
≤500 chars/value — VO `Metadata`), `tags` (lista de slugs, ≤40 c/u, ≤30 — VO
`DocumentTagCollection`), `custom_fields` (lista tipada `[{field,value}]`,
`field` ≤60, `value` ≤500, ≤50 — VO `CustomFields`).
CreateQuoteRequest.title: CreateQuoteRequest

CreateRecurringInvoiceRequest.description:
Public REST API v1 — POST /v1/recurring_invoices.

Required body: `client_id`, `series_id`, `frequency`,
`start_on`, `lines[]` (min 1). Optional: `end_on`, `name`,
`description`, `notes`, `metadata` (≤50 keys, ≤500 chars/value —
VO `Metadata`), `holiday_handling`, `days_before_due`,
`max_occurrences`, `email_to`, `send_automatically`. Each line accepts
`description`, `quantity`, `unit_price`, `tax_rate`, `retention`,
`surcharge` (rates 0-100).

Frequency v1 (contractual whitelist):
`daily|weekly|biweekly|monthly|quarterly|semiannual|yearly`.
`yearly` is mapped to `annual` (internal RecurringInvoice BC) in the
controller.

By default the recurrence is born `active` with `frequency_interval=1`.
The fields `holiday_handling` (default `same`), `days_before_due`
(default `30`), `max_occurrences` (default ∞), `email_to` (default none)
and `send_automatically` (default `true`) ARE configurable in the body;
if omitted, those defaults apply.

`holiday_handling` accepts `skip|same|before|after`. The value validation
is performed by the VO `HolidayHandling::create()` in the Handler (throws
`InvalidHolidayHandlingException` → 422 `invalid_holiday_handling`); it is
not duplicated here with an `in:` rule, to preserve the canonical catalog subcode.

`tags` (lista de slugs, ≤40 c/u, ≤30 — VO `DocumentTagCollection`) y
`custom_fields` (lista tipada `[{field,value}]`, `field` ≤60, `value` ≤500,
≤50 — VO `CustomFields`) viven a nivel de la RECURRENCIA (cabecera), no de
las facturas generadas (change `document-ergonomics-and-tags` §11).
CreateRecurringInvoiceRequest.title: CreateRecurringInvoiceRequest

CreateSeriesRequest.description:
Public REST API v1 — POST /v1/series.

Required body: `code` (string, max 10 — invariant of the VO `SeriesCode`),
`document_type` (internal enum whitelist `SeriesType`). Optional: `name`,
`prefix`, `year_reset` (boolean), `number_format` (canonical mask string),
`initial_number` (integer >= 1).

`code`, `name` and `year_reset` are honored and materialized by the `Series`
aggregate (enables multi-series with distinct codes per type). `prefix`
is an OUTPUT alias of `code` in the Resource — it is not mapped to the Command.

The `max:10` limit of `code` must match the invariant of
`SeriesCode::create()` (throws if empty or > 10 chars). Without this
alignment, an 11+ char code would pass validation and blow up in the VO
with an HTTP 500 instead of the expected 422.

`number_format` accepts the canonical mask (e.g. `{code}-{YYYY}-{000}`) and
is validated against the closed grammar of the `NumberFormat` VO via
`withValidator()`: the VO itself is the authority (padding `1..10`, year
token `{YYYY, YY, NONE}`, separator `{-, /, ., _}`). A malformed mask yields
`422` with the Spanish message of `InvalidNumberFormatException` — NEVER a
`500` from the VO blowing up downstream. If omitted, the BC applies the
canonical default (`{code}-{YYYY}-{000}`, padding 3).

`initial_number` (`nullable|integer|min:1`) lets an integrator continue an
existing numbering when migrating (the domain already supports it
end-to-end via `YearCounter::create(initialNumber)`; this request just
wires the v1 surface that previously ignored it). If omitted, the counter
starts at `1`.

Public `document_type` whitelist: `invoice, quote, delivery_note,
proforma`. The `contract` type is outside the public v1 surface
(it is not a billable document exposed to integrators) even though the
domain VO `SeriesType` still supports it for the SPA. `purchase_invoice`
and `recurring_invoice` are not yet supported by the internal BC and are
rejected in validation — they will be added additively when the domain supports them.

Unknown input fields are SILENTLY IGNORED (current contract, not hardened
in this change): the controller reads only the named fields above via
`$request->input(...)`, and any body key absent from `rules()` is dropped
by Laravel's `validated()` — it never reaches the `Series` aggregate.
Integrators must stick to the documented fields; extra keys are no-ops, not
422 errors.
CreateSeriesRequest.title: CreateSeriesRequest

CreateSupplierRequest.description:
Public REST API v1 — POST /v1/suppliers.

Required body: `name`. Optional: `tax_id`, `vat_id`, `email`, `phone`,
`address` (object with sub-keys `line1`, `line2`, `number`, `floor`,
`door`, `staircase`, `postal_code`, `city`, `province`, `country`),
`notes`, `metadata`, `business_name`, `commercial_name`, `contact_person`,
`fax`, `mobile`, `website`, `billing_emails[]`, `default_discount`,
`default_vat_rate`, `default_retention_rate`, `is_surcharge_subject`,
`accumulate_347`, `bank_accounts[]`, `iban` (flat legacy alias),
`coordinates`, `preferred_operation_regime`, `payment_method`,
`payment_terms_days`, `alternative_id` (object `{type, value, country_code}`),
`default_taxes_id`.

The validation of `metadata` invariants (≤50 keys, ≤500 chars/value) is
performed by the Handler via `Metadata::create()`. The validation of domain
invariants (XOR `tax_id`/`alternative_id`, direct_debit ⇒ default bank
account, billing_emails without duplicates, IBAN format) is performed by the
`Supplier` aggregate and the VOs (`Metadata`, `BankAccount`). The typed
exceptions (`MetadataTooManyKeysException`, `MetadataValueTooLongException`,
`InvalidTaxIdException`, `InvalidAlternativeIdException`,
`InvalidBillingEmailsException`, `InvalidBankAccountException`) implement
`PublicApiMappable` and propagate to the `ExceptionRenderer` with the
canonical v1 envelope.

`tax_id` is relaxed to `nullable` on this endpoint; the aggregate requires
`tax_id XOR alternative_id`.
CreateSupplierRequest.title: CreateSupplierRequest

CreateTaxRequest.description:
Public REST API v1 — POST /v1/taxes.

Creates a custom tax with name, unique code, type
(`vat|retention|surcharge|other`), rate and scope
(`sale|purchase|both`). The ISO-2 country code (`country`) is
required.

Accepts the 7 optional AEAT/B2B extend fields
(`customer_visible_label`, `external_reference`, `valid_from`,
`valid_until`, `reverse_charge`, `country_aeat_zone`, `metadata`)
and the consolidated map `default_for_documents`. The uniqueness
validation of `code` is delegated to the aggregate via
`DuplicateTaxCodeException` (409).

customer_visible_label.description:
===== EXTEND AEAT/B2B =====
NOTE: no `max:200` rule here. The 200-char limit is enforced by the
VO `CustomerVisibleLabel::create()` via
`InvalidCustomerVisibleLabelException::tooLong(200)` with the canonical
subcode `invalid_customer_visible_label` (spec error catalog §24).
With `max:200` the FormRequest would emit `invalid_param_value` and the
domain subcode would never materialize.

valid_until.description:
NOTE: no `after_or_equal:valid_from` rule here. The invariant
`valid_until >= valid_from` is enforced by the Aggregate via
`InvalidValidityWindowException::endBeforeStart()` with the canonical
subcode `invalid_validity_window` (spec error catalog §6).

CreateTaxRequest.title: CreateTaxRequest

CreateWebhookEndpointRequest.description:
POST /v1/webhook_endpoints

Body:
  - `url`            required, HTTPS, max 2048
  - `enabled_events` required, non-empty array, elements from the public
                     event catalog (`EventName::publicCatalog()`): the closed
                     active catalog minus events whose payment gateway is not
                     yet released — mirrors the v1 route / MCP tool lockdown.
                     Only `available` events are subscribable. Events in
                     `EventName::RESERVED` (status `coming_soon`, currently
                     empty) are LISTED in `GET /v1/event-catalog` but are NOT
                     yet emitted, so they are NOT subscribable: sending one in
                     `enabled_events` yields 422 (`enabled_events.N`). For
                     compatibility, the alias `events` is also accepted: if
                     the client sends `events` and does not send
                     `enabled_events`, we promote it in `prepareForValidation`.
  - `description`    optional, max 255
  - `ip_allowlist`   optional, array of strings (plain IP or CIDR)
  - `api_version`    optional, `YYYY-MM-DD` format and a supported value
  - `timeout_seconds` optional, integer between 1 and 30 (default 10)
CreateWebhookEndpointRequest.title: CreateWebhookEndpointRequest

CustomField.description: A typed custom field as an ordered `{field, value}` pair. Distinct from `metadata` (a free key→value map): use `custom_fields` for structured, display-oriented integration metadata.
CustomField.title: CustomField

CustomHeaders.description: Custom HTTP headers (string → string) added to every delivery request for a webhook endpoint. Max 20 entries, each value ≤ 1024 chars. Header names are normalized to Title-Case. Reserved names (`Host`, `Content-Type`, `Factuarea-Signature`, `X-Forwarded-*`, …) are rejected. Empty object when none are set. Never put secrets here — values are returned verbatim by the API.
CustomHeaders.title: CustomHeaders

DeclaracionResponsable.description: The producer-level VeriFactu Declaración Responsable (SIF compliance declaration issued by the system producer, Factuarea). Global per `version`, read-only — not per-company.

id.description: UUID (v7) of the declaration.

system_id.description: Identifier of the invoicing software system (SIF).

system_name.description: Name of the invoicing software system.

verifactu_only.description: Indicates whether the system operates only in VeriFactu mode (tipo_uso = "S").

signing_place.description: Place where the declaration is signed.

content_hash.description: SHA-256 hash of the declaration content for integrity verification (the full content is not exposed).

DeclaracionResponsable.title: DeclaracionResponsable

DeliveryNote.description: A delivery note tracking goods delivered to a customer.

status.description: Delivery note lifecycle status exposed by the public API: `draft` (created, editable), `sent` (goods marked as delivered, no signature recorded yet), `signed` (delivered AND a recipient signature has been recorded), `invoiced` (converted into an invoice, terminal), `cancelled` (terminal). Note: the internal `delivered` state is surfaced as `sent` (without signature) or `signed` (with signature) — there is no `delivered` value in the public API. Recording a signature on a `sent` note moves it to `signed` (it does not introduce a brand-new lifecycle stage; the signature presence is the only difference).

vehicle_plate.description: License plate of the delivery vehicle.

driver.description: Delivery driver data. `null` when no sub-field is provided.

tracking.description: Shipment tracking data. `null` when no sub-field is provided.

name.description: Name of the person who received the goods.

received_by.description: Goods recipient data. `null` when no sub-field is provided.

billing_emails.description: Additional emails for delivery note dispatch (administration, accounting). Maximum 5. Empty `[]` when there are none.

external_id.description: External integration key (ERP/CRM/e-commerce) mapping this document to a record in a third-party system. Free-format, unique per company, filterable via `?external_id=`. `null` when not set. Persistent synchronization key, independent of the request-level `Idempotency-Key`.

tags.description: Free classification tags (lowercase slugs `[a-z0-9-]`, ≤ 40 chars each, ≤ 30 tags). Filterable via `?tags[in]=tag1,tag2` (JSON_CONTAINS, OR semantics). Empty `[]` when there are none.

custom_fields.description: Ordered list of typed custom fields `[{field, value}]` (≤ 50). Distinct from `metadata` (a free key→value map): use `custom_fields` for structured, display-oriented integration metadata. Empty `[]` when there are none.

converted_to_id.description: UUID (v7) of the invoice this delivery note was converted into.

DeliveryNote.title: DeliveryNote

DeliveryNoteLine.description: A line item on a delivery note.

description.example: Cajas de material A4 (500u)

retention_rate.description: IRPF withholding percentage applied to the line (0–100). Default 0. Its amount is already aggregated into `taxes`.

surcharge_rate.description: Equivalence surcharge (recargo de equivalencia) percentage applied to the line (0–100). Default 0. Its amount is already aggregated into `taxes`.

DeliveryNoteLine.title: DeliveryNoteLine

DeliveryNoteStats.description: Resumen agregado de los albaranes de la empresa autenticada: total, importe acumulado, desglose por estado interno, pendientes de firma y convertidos a factura este mes. Devuelto por `GET /v1/delivery_notes/stats`.

total_count.description: Total number of recorded delivery notes.

by_status.description: Breakdown by internal delivery note state. The key is the internal state (`draft`, `delivered`, `invoiced`, `cancelled`); the value is an object with `status`, `count` and `total` (accumulated amount in EUR). States with no delivery notes do not appear.

status.description: Internal state of the delivery note.

count.description: Number of delivery notes in that state.

total.description: Importe acumulado de los albaranes en ese estado (EUR).

pending_signature.description: Delivered delivery notes (`delivered`) that do not yet have a recorded signature.

converted_this_month.description: Albaranes convertidos a factura (`invoiced`) durante el mes en curso.

DeliveryNoteStats.title: DeliveryNoteStats

DeliveryNoteStatusItem.description: Delivery note status from the closed catalog with its label and color for UI.

color.description: Color sugerido para pintar el estado en UI.

DeliveryNoteStatusItem.title: DeliveryNoteStatusItem

code.description: Stable error code, e.g. missing_api_key, insufficient_scope, parameter_invalid, invalid_status_transition.

subcode.description: Sub-code detailing the subtype within `code`. Only present on some 409 errors — e.g. `code=resource_already_exists`, `subcode=tax_id_already_exists` to indicate exactly which duplicate key triggered the conflict.

message.example: No se ha proporcionado una API key válida en el header Authorization.
examples: No se ha proporcionado una API key válida en el header Authorization.

param.description: Field that caused the error (for parameter_invalid and similar invalid_request_error codes).

doc_url.description: Link to error reference in docs.

Event.description: A read-only record of something that happened in your account. The same object is delivered to your webhook endpoints.

id.description: Opaque identifier of the event, a UUID v7 (e.g. `01931b3e-7c4a-7f2e-9a8b-3c5d6e7f8a9b`). Consistent with the `id` field of every other v1 resource.

type.description: Event type in `<category>.<action>` form (e.g. `invoice.paid`, `quote.approved`). See `GET /event-catalog` for the full list.

aggregate_id.description: UUID v7 of the resource that produced this event (e.g. the invoice for `invoice.paid`). `null` for events without backfill. Distinct from `id`, which identifies the event itself.

correlation_id.description: UUID v7 correlating this event end-to-end with the operation that produced it. `null` for events without a correlation context (e.g. born from scheduler sweeps). The key is always present.

api_version.description: API version (date-based) the payload was serialized under, sealed at emission (e.g. `2026-05-22`). `null` only for legacy events emitted before versions were sealed.

livemode.description: `true` for events generated in production (live keys, `fact_live_`); `false` for events generated in test mode (sandbox company, test keys `fact_test_`).

created.description: Unix timestamp (seconds) of when the event was created.

Event.title: Event

EventCatalogEntry.description: A single event type available for webhook subscriptions. The full catalog is returned by `GET /v1/event-catalog`.

category.description: High-level group derived from the prefix (`invoice`, `quote`, `client`, …).

description.example: Factura pagada

status.description: `available` if the event is emitted today; `coming_soon` if reserved for a future release.

EventCatalogEntry.title: EventCatalogEntry

EventData.description: The payload embedded when the event was emitted, discriminated by `type`. Carries the full snapshot of the affected resource under `object` — identical to `GET /v1/<resource>/{id}` at emission time — plus event-specific keys, e.g. `{ "type": "invoice.paid", "object": { ...invoice... }, "amount": 1210.0 }`. The snapshot is frozen: later changes to the resource do not rewrite past events.

EventDeletedObject.title: EventDeletedObject

EventPaymentSnapshot.description: Snapshot of a received payment captured at emission time. Payments have no dedicated v1 endpoint; this shape only appears inside `payment.*` events.

invoice_id.description: Identifier of the invoice the payment was applied to.

EventPaymentSnapshot.title: EventPaymentSnapshot

FaceSubmission.description: A submission of an invoice to FACe (the Spanish B2G general entry point). Tracks the FACe registry number and the processing status reported by the FACe web service.

id.description: UUID (v7) of the FACe submission.

status.description: FACe processing status (`submitted`, `registered_rcf`, `accounted`, `paid`, `rejected`, `cancellation_requested`, `cancelled`, `error`). Kept up to date by the system polling — there is no refresh endpoint in v1.

registry_number.description: Registry number assigned by FACe on presentation. Synthetic `FACE-SANDBOX-*` for sandbox (test key) submissions.

dir3_accounting_office.description: DIR3 code of the accounting office (oficina contable, role 01) snapshotted at submission time.

dir3_managing_body.description: DIR3 code of the managing body (órgano gestor, role 02) snapshotted at submission time.

dir3_processing_unit.description: DIR3 code of the processing unit (unidad tramitadora, role 03) snapshotted at submission time.

error_code.description: Local transmission error code, `null` unless the submission is in `error` state.

error_message.description: Human-readable transmission error message (in Spanish), `null` unless the submission is in `error` state.

status_updated_at.description: When the processing status last changed (ISO 8601).

last_polled_at.description: When the system last polled FACe for this submission, or `null` if never polled yet.

FaceSubmission.title: FaceSubmission

FindClientByExternalIdRequest.description:
Public REST API v1 — POST /v1/clients/find-by-external-id.

Busca un cliente por su `external_id` (clave de integración ERP/CRM) dentro
de la company autenticada.
FindClientByExternalIdRequest.title: FindClientByExternalIdRequest

FindClientByTaxIdRequest.description:
Public REST API v1 — POST /v1/clients/find-by-tax-id.

Busca un cliente por su identificador fiscal (NIF/CIF/NIE) dentro de la
company autenticada.
FindClientByTaxIdRequest.title: FindClientByTaxIdRequest

FindDeliveryNoteByExternalIdRequest.description:
Public REST API v1 — POST /v1/delivery_notes/find-by-external-id.

Busca un albarán por su `external_id` (clave de integración ERP/CRM)
dentro de la company autenticada.
FindDeliveryNoteByExternalIdRequest.title: FindDeliveryNoteByExternalIdRequest

FindInvoiceByExternalIdRequest.description:
Public REST API v1 — POST /v1/invoices/find-by-external-id.

Busca una factura por su `external_id` (clave de integración ERP/CRM) dentro
de la company autenticada.
FindInvoiceByExternalIdRequest.title: FindInvoiceByExternalIdRequest

FindInvoiceByNumberRequest.description:
Public REST API v1 — POST /v1/invoices/find-by-number.

Body: `number` (invoice number, required), `year` optional to
disambiguate when the number repeats across fiscal years. Aligned with
Supplier's `find-by-tax-id`.
FindInvoiceByNumberRequest.title: FindInvoiceByNumberRequest

FindProductByExternalIdRequest.description:
Public REST API v1 — POST /v1/products/find-by-external-id.

Busca un producto por su `external_id` (clave de integración ERP/CRM de
terceros) dentro de la company autenticada. El `external_id` viaja en el body
(no en la URL) — paralelo a `find-by-sku` — porque una clave externa puede
contener caracteres `.` y `/` válidos que romperían el path, y para no
exponerla en logs/URLs. Ortogonal a `find-by-sku`.
FindProductByExternalIdRequest.title: FindProductByExternalIdRequest

FindProductBySkuRequest.description:
Public REST API v1 — POST /v1/products/find-by-sku.

Looks up a product by its `sku` within the authenticated company. The `sku`
travels in the body (not in the URL) so as not to expose SKUs in logs/URLs
and because a SKU may contain valid `.` and `/` characters that would break
the path.
FindProductBySkuRequest.title: FindProductBySkuRequest

FindProformaByExternalIdRequest.description:
Public REST API v1 — POST /v1/proformas/find-by-external-id.

Busca una proforma por su `external_id` (clave de integración ERP/CRM)
dentro de la company autenticada.
FindProformaByExternalIdRequest.title: FindProformaByExternalIdRequest

FindPurchaseInvoiceByExternalIdRequest.description:
Public REST API v1 — POST /v1/purchase_invoices/find-by-external-id.

Busca una factura de compra por su `external_id` (clave de integración
ERP/CRM) dentro de la company autenticada. Ortogonal a
`external_invoice_number` (el número fiscal del proveedor).
FindPurchaseInvoiceByExternalIdRequest.title: FindPurchaseInvoiceByExternalIdRequest

FindQuoteByExternalIdRequest.description:
Public REST API v1 — POST /v1/quotes/find-by-external-id.

Busca un presupuesto por su `external_id` (clave de integración ERP/CRM)
dentro de la company autenticada.
FindQuoteByExternalIdRequest.title: FindQuoteByExternalIdRequest

FindRecordByAeatCsvV1Request.description:
Public REST API v1 — POST /v1/verifactu/records/find-by-csv.

Body `{"aeat_csv": "X-Y-Z"}`. Canonical V1 validation (NEVER a generic
`validation_error`/422):
 - `aeat_csv` missing            → 400 `parameter_missing` (param `aeat_csv`).
 - mutually exclusive fields     → 400 `parameter_unknown` (subcode
   `mutually_exclusive_query_params`).

The handler normalizes (`str_replace('-', '', strtoupper($csv))`) before the
query (BR-VFC-016). The 404 is emitted via `RecordNotFoundException::withAeatCsv()`.
FindRecordByAeatCsvV1Request.title: FindRecordByAeatCsvV1Request

FindRecordByHuellaV1Request.description:
Public REST API v1 — POST /v1/verifactu/records/find-by-huella.

Body `{"huella": "<64-char-hex>"}`. Canonical V1 validation (NEVER a generic
`validation_error`/422):
 - `huella` missing              → 400 `parameter_missing` (param `huella`).
 - mutually exclusive fields     → 400 `parameter_unknown` (subcode
   `mutually_exclusive_query_params`) if `aeat_csv`, `series` or `number`
   appear together with `huella`.
 - invalid huella format         → 422 `business_rule_violation`
   (`InvalidHuellaException`, VO invariant; param `huella`).

The 404 (huella not found, multi-tenant) is emitted by the handler via
`RecordNotFoundException::withHuella()` (param `huella`).
FindRecordByHuellaV1Request.title: FindRecordByHuellaV1Request

FindRecordByInvoiceNumberV1Request.description:
Public REST API v1 — POST /v1/verifactu/records/find-by-invoice-number.

Body `{"series": "F", "number": "2026-019"}`. Canonical V1 validation
(NEVER a generic `validation_error`/422):
 - `series` or `number` missing  → 400 `parameter_missing`.
 - mutually exclusive fields     → 400 `parameter_unknown` (subcode
   `mutually_exclusive_query_params`) if `huella` or `aeat_csv` are present.

The 404 is emitted by the handler via `RecordNotFoundException::withInvoiceNumber()`.
FindRecordByInvoiceNumberV1Request.title: FindRecordByInvoiceNumberV1Request

FindSeriesByCodeRequest.description:
